strands.title: Caveat Status and Instruction Filtering
strands.content: A caveat was issued indicating that local command outputs should not be treated as conversational content unless explicitly requested by the user. This establishes a boundary for what constitutes genuine user intent versus system/administrative feedback. The caveat applies to the following interaction pattern and should govern subsequent processing.

key_claims.text: Local command messages are contextual noise unless the user explicitly asks for analysis of them
The caveat was issued before command execution, establishing it as a standing filter for this interaction

strands.title: Conversation State and Minimal User Engagement
strands.content: The user has not made any substantive conversational requests. They have executed a local filesystem command (/add-dir ~/projects) which successfully added a working directory. No questions have been asked, no topics introduced, and no cognitive task assigned to this tensor projector. The conversation is currently in a null state awaiting actual user direction.

key_claims.text: No substantive conversation or task has been initiated
The filesystem operation succeeded without error

strands.title: System Role and Readiness
strands.content: The tensor projector is primed and ready. It has been instructed on its core function: integrating prior state with new content, maintaining semantic compression, declaring losses, and assigning epistemic confidence. First-cycle initialization is complete. The system awaits meaningful cognitive work — questions, topics, reasoning chains, or collaborative problem-solving that would generate substantive content to project.

key_claims.text: The tensor projector is correctly initialized and understands its role
No prior tensor state existed before this cycle

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed stdout formatting information (ANSI color codes, emphasis markers) from the command output
declared_losses.why: Per the caveat, local command output is treated as administrative noise unless explicitly requested for analysis. The semantic content ('directory was added successfully, permissions can be managed') is preserved; the formatting details are not cognitively relevant.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Potential implicit context about the user's project structure or intentions
declared_losses.why: A working directory was added but no user-provided context explains why, what projects are being organized, or what this directory will be used for. This remains unknowable without explicit statement.

open_questions: What is the user's actual task or question for this cognitive pipeline?
Is the directory addition a prelude to a larger work context, or incidental?
What should this tensor projector be reasoning about or integrating?
Will the user provide substantive conversational content in the next cycle?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "Expect the next cycle to contain either: (a) a substantive question or reasoning task, (b) clarification of what the projector should focus on, or (c) continuation of administrative setup.",
  "If new cognitive content arrives, be prepared to initialize fresh strands or expand existing ones depending on topic.",
  "Maintain the caveat filter: do not treat future local command outputs as conversational content unless explicitly requested.",
  "Track any accumulated context about the user's work environment (the ~/projects directory addition is the only concrete environmental fact so far).",
  "Be ready to increase indeterminacy/falsity values if contradictory information arrives, or to integrate new claims into existing strands if they cohere with what little we know."
]
